verif_result.fix_hint: Simulation completed but values disagreed. max_error=29 across 65536 samples. See first_mismatch_* and expected/got for raw evidence.
verif_result.verilator_stderr: Simulation completed but values disagreed. max_error=29 across 65536 samples. See first_mismatch_* and expected/got for raw evidence.
verif_result.classifier_reason: Simulation completed with correct timing (1 cycle) and correct output count (2048/2048), but all 31727 mismatches have negative signed error (got < expected, signed_error_sum=-165761, positive_error_count=0). The ratio got/expected is consistently ~0.714 across all non-zero values (e.g., 12/17, 5/7, 15/21, 46/64). This is a scale factor bug: 5/7 ≈ 0.714 ≈ 1/1.4, consistent with the relu applying the wrong requantization rescale—likely missing the input_scale/output_scale division (scale_factor=0.09857 is present in the spec but not applied, or applied with wrong shift). This is the same relu-rescale class documented in project memory (apply_relu_rescale.py fix). No resource, bus width, or toolchain issues are present; a targeted RTL fix to the requantization path will resolve it.
